title: NOC 12101 – Human Resources and Recruitment Officers | GTR Immigration
description: Human Resources and Recruitment Officers are essential to workforce planning and talent acquisition across organizations in Canada. Under NOC 12101, these professionals benefit from steady hiring demand and practical pathways toward Canadian Permanent Residence (PR) through skilled worker immigration programs.

classification.teer: 2 – Occupations usually requiring a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more years; or supervisory occupations

duties: Human Resources and Recruitment Officers may:
Identify staffing requirements and post job advertisements
Screen applications and advise candidates on employment terms
Recruit graduates from colleges and universities
Participate in selection boards and evaluation processes
Prepare job offers and notify applicants of outcomes
Coordinate training and employee assistance programs
Manage Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) files
employment_requirements: A college diploma in business administration, industrial relations, commerce, or a related field
Completion of a professional development program in personnel administration
CHRP certification may be required by some employers
Prior clerical or administrative HR experience may be necessary
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 11,900 new positions for Human Resources and Recruitment Officers, while approximately 14,300 job seekers are expected to enter the field. With the right immigration strategy and regional targeting, internationally trained professionals can still secure strong opportunities.
additional_information: Human Resources and Recruitment Officers are widely employed in both public institutions and private-sector firms, particularly in corporate HR teams and recruiting agencies.

Growing hiring needs across industriesStrong post-pandemic recovery in staffing functionsEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Employer-supported immigration routesAnnual earnings typically ranging from $38,500 to $93,800
